name: Utena County (Lithuania)

colors.symbolism: the standard outer field of Lithuanian county flags
the field of the Utena coat of arms

description: A blue field with a red inner panel bearing the coat of arms of Utena County in the centre.
history: Like the flags of all ten Lithuanian counties, Utena County's flag follows the uniform template introduced after the country's redivision into apskritys (counties) in 1994: a blue outer border surrounding the county's heraldic field, here red, charged with the county arms.

features.symbolism: blue outer border in the Lithuanian county pattern, with a red inner field bearing the county arms

features.role: coat of arms of Utena County
features.symbolism: the county's heraldic device, referencing Utena's regional and historical identity
